subject: HTTP protocol block key in templates
new: http:
truth: Replace the template block key 'requests:' with 'http:' for HTTP protocol definitions.
quote: The HTTP protocol block key must be updated from `requests:` to `http:`.

subject: Network/TCP protocol block key in templates
new: tcp:
truth: Replace the template block key 'network:' with 'tcp:' for TCP/network protocol definitions.
quote: `network:` -> `tcp:` (TCP/network protocol block key)

subject: Support for deprecated template paths
truth: Stop using deprecated template paths; update all template path references before v2.9.5 or they will fail to load.
quote: "Found N templates loaded with deprecated paths, update before v2.9.5 for continued support"

subject: Support for deprecated protocol syntax in templates
truth: Stop using old protocol syntax in templates; update to new syntax before v2.9.5 or templates will fail to load.
quote: "Found N templates loaded with deprecated protocol syntax, update before v2.9.5 for continued support"

subject: execution model
old: implicit sequential execution of multiple requests
new: explicit `flow:` field using JavaScript (ECMAScript 5.1 via goja) to orchestrate/conditionally execute requests
truth: Stop relying on implicit sequential request execution; you must now use the explicit `flow:` field to orchestrate request execution order.
quote: v2 used implicit sequential execution of multiple requests. v3 adds an explicit `flow:` field using JavaScript (ECMAScript 5.1 via goja) to orchestrate/conditionally execute requests, e.g. `flow: http(1) && http(2)`.

from_fact: implicit sequential execution of multiple requests
explicit `flow:` field using javascript (ecmascript 5.1 via goja) to orchestrate/conditionally execute requests

subject: command-line flag -fuzz
new: -dast
truth: Stop using `-fuzz`; use `-dast` instead.
quote: `-fuzz`  ->  `-dast`            (`-fuzz` still loads fuzzing/DAST templates but is Deprecated: use -dast)

subject: -cup / -cloud-upload flag
new: -dashboard / -pd
truth: Stop using -cloud-upload (-cup); use -dashboard (-pd) instead to upload scan results to the cloud dashboard.
quote: -cup, -cloud-upload             upload scan results to pdcp dashboard [DEPRECATED use -dashboard]

subject: command-line flag -rlm / -rate-limit-minute
new: -rl / -rate-limit
truth: Stop using `-rlm` or `-rate-limit-minute`; use `-rl` or `-rate-limit` instead.
quote: `-rlm` / `-rate-limit-minute`  ->  DEPRECATED (use `-rl` / `-rate-limit`)

subject: include-rr flag
new: -omit-raw
truth: Stop using -include-rr (deprecated); use -omit-raw instead, and note the default behaviour has inverted (include-rr defaulted true, omit-raw defaults to false meaning request/response are now included by default).
quote: -irr, -include-rr -omit-raw   include request/response pairs in the JSON, JSONL, and Markdown outputs (for findings only) [DEPRECATED use -omit-raw] (default true)

subject: command-line flag -ztls
new: autofallback to ztls enabled by default
truth: Stop using `-ztls` flag; ztls autofallback is now enabled by default.
quote: `-ztls`  ->  Deprecated (autofallback to ztls enabled by default)

from_fact: autofallback to ztls enabled by default

subject: redirects field
new: redirects defined per template, not per request
truth: The `redirects` field is now defined per template, not per request block.
quote: Currently redirects are defined per template, not per request.

from_fact: redirects defined per template, not per request

subject: exclude-ports field overwrites reserved list
new: exclude-ports field overwrites the default reserved ports list entirely
truth: Setting exclude-ports in a network template completely overwrites the default reserved ports list, so any port not in exclude-ports will be treated as non-reserved and will use the user-supplied port.
quote: When `exclude-ports` is used, the default reserved ports list will be overwritten. This means that if you want to run a network template on port `80`, you will have to explicitly specify it in the port field.

from_fact: exclude-ports field overwrites the default reserved ports list entirely

subject: port field supports comma-separated values
old: port field only accepted a single port value
new: port field accepts comma-separated multi-port values
truth: Starting from Nuclei v3.1.0, the port field supports comma-separated values, and Nuclei will check which ports are open from the list and run the template only on open ports.
quote: Starting from Nuclei v3.1.0 `port` field supports comma seperated values and multi ports can be specified in the port field.

from_fact: port field only accepted a single port value
port field accepts comma-separated multi-port values

subject: network protocol data field names
new: request, data
truth: Stop using 'raw', 'body', or 'all' as field names in network protocol templates; use 'request' for the network request and 'data' for the final data read from the network socket instead.
quote:
| Value            | Description                         |
| ---------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| request          | Network Request                     |
| data             | Final Data Read From Network Socket |
| raw / body / all | All Data received from Socket       |

subject: nuclei JS bindings - protocol execution function
old: Calling a protocol like `ssl()` or `http()` in JavaScript executed the protocol.
new: Calling a protocol like `ssl()` or `http()` in JavaScript now requires passing index or ID arguments to execute specific requests.
truth: Protocol execution functions (e.g., `ssl()`, `http()`) now require an index or ID argument to execute a specific request; calling them without arguments may no longer work or may behave differently.
quote: If you want to execute a specific request of a protocol (refer to nuclei-flow-dns for an example), it can be achieved by passing either: * The index of that request in the protocol (e.g.,`dns(1)`, `dns(2)`) * The ID of that request in the protocol (e.g., `dns("extract-vps")`, `http("probe-http")`)

from_fact: calling a protocol like `ssl()` or `http()` in javascript executed the protocol.
calling a protocol like `ssl()` or `http()` in javascript now requires passing index or id arguments to execute specific requests.

subject: nuclei
old: Filters applied on installed path of templates by default
new: Filters are applied on installed path of templates and can be customized with manual template path input
truth: Filters now require explicit template path (-t) when used with -tags; they no longer automatically scan all installed templates unless -t is also provided.
quote: As default, Filters are applied on installed path of templates and can be customized with manual template path input.

from_fact: filters applied on installed path of templates by default
filters are applied on installed path of templates and can be customized with manual template path input

subject: AI template generation flag
new: -ai flag for AI-powered template generation
truth: The `-ai` flag is now used to generate templates via AI, requires internet and a ProjectDiscovery API key, and is limited to 100 queries per day.
quote:
The `-ai` flag requires an active internet connection to communicate with the ProjectDiscovery API. Generated templates are stored both locally on your computer and in your ProjectDiscovery cloud account for future reference. For privacy, your prompts and generated templates are not used for AI training.
Currently, each user is limited to 100 AI template generation queries per day.

subject: -metrics port
truth: Stop using the separate -metrics port; metrics are now served on the -stats port at localhost:9092/metrics when -stats is enabled.
quote: From Nuclei v3.0.0 `-metrics` port has been removed and merged with `-stats` when using `-stats` flag metrics will be by default available at `localhost:9092/metrics` and metrics-port can be configured by `-metrics-port` flag

subject: .nuclei-ignore file
old: Users could modify .nuclei-ignore file to customize ignore list
new: .nuclei-ignore file is internal and must not be modified by users; use nuclei configuration file instead
truth: Stop modifying .nuclei-ignore directly; use the nuclei configuration file to overwrite the ignore list.
quote: **.nuclei-ignore** file is not supposed to be modified by user, as it gets used by nuclei internally, to overwrite ignore list, utilize [nuclei configuration](/opensource/nuclei/running#nuclei-config) file.

from_fact: users could modify .nuclei-ignore file to customize ignore list
.nuclei-ignore file is internal and must not be modified by users; use nuclei configuration file instead

subject: nuclei config file default path
new: $HOME/.config/nuclei/config.yaml
truth: The default config file path is now $HOME/.config/nuclei/config.yaml.
quote: Default path of nuclei config file is $HOME/.config/nuclei/config.yaml

subject: PDCP API key configuration
old: API key was entered via interactive prompt
new: API key must be set via PDCP_API_KEY environment variable or credentials file
truth: PDCP API key must now be set via the PDCP_API_KEY environment variable or the credentials file at $HOME/.pdcp/credentials.yaml instead of an interactive prompt.
quote: Avoid entering your API key via interactive prompt by setting it via environment variable.

from_fact: api key was entered via interactive prompt
api key must be set via pdcp_api_key environment variable or credentials file

subject: Jira custom field configuration
old: Using `name` as the dropdown value and `id` as the ID value of the dropdown
new: `name` is the dropdown value, `id` is the ID value of the dropdown, `freeform` is for text entry
truth: Stop using `name` as the dropdown ID; `name` is now the dropdown display value, `id` is the dropdown ID, and `freeform` is for free-text custom fields.
quote:
`name` is the dropdown value
* `id` is the ID value of the dropdown
* `freeform` is if the customfield the entry of any value

from_fact: using `name` as the dropdown value and `id` as the id value of the dropdown
`name` is the dropdown value, `id` is the id value of the dropdown, `freeform` is for text entry

subject: Jira authentication fields
new: Using `account-id` and `token` for Jira Cloud, or `account-id` as username and `token` as password for on-prem
truth: Stop using `username`/`password` fields; use `account-id` (username for on-prem) and `token` (password for on-prem) instead.
quote: # account-id is the account-id of the Jira user or username in case of on-prem Jira

from_fact: using `account-id` and `token` for jira cloud, or `account-id` as username and `token` as password for on-prem

subject: Jira duplicate detection status field
old: Using `CLOSED_STATUS` variable or a field named `closed-status`
new: Using `status-not` variable in the Jira template
truth: Stop using `CLOSED_STATUS`; use the `status-not` variable in the Jira template file to set the closed status for duplicate detection.
quote: The `CLOSED_STATUS` can be changed in the Jira template file using the `status-not` variable.

from_fact: using `closed_status` variable or a field named `closed-status`
using `status-not` variable in the jira template

subject: Jira severity field configuration
old: Using `severity-as-label` as the only way to send severity
new: `severity-as-label` is optional; user custom fields for Jira Cloud are recommended instead
truth: Stop relying solely on `severity-as-label`; use Jira Cloud custom fields for severity instead, as `severity-as-label` is now optional.
quote:
SeverityAsLabel (optional) sends the severity as the label of the created issue
# User custom fields for Jira Cloud instead

from_fact: using `severity-as-label` as the only way to send severity
`severity-as-label` is optional; user custom fields for jira cloud are recommended instead

subject: running all nuclei templates
old: running all templates by default includes fuzzing/DoS templates
new: fuzzing/DoS templates are excluded from default scan and require -itags flag
truth: Fuzzing and DoS templates are excluded from the default scan and can only be executed when explicitly invoked using the -itags option.
quote: To ensure these templates are not accidentally run, they are tagged and excluded them from the default scan. These templates can be only executed when explicitly invoked using the `-itags` option.

from_fact: running all templates by default includes fuzzing/dos templates
fuzzing/dos templates are excluded from default scan and require -itags flag

subject: nuclei engine behaviour for multi-protocol/flow templates
old: Before v3.1.4, multi-protocol templates with `flow` only returned one result
new: From v3.1.4, nuclei prints all events/results in a template
truth: From v3.1.4, nuclei prints all events/results in multi-protocol/flow templates instead of only one result, and template writers must use `internal: true` on matchers to suppress intermediate results.
quote: Before nuclei v3.1.4 , A template like [`CVE-2023-43177`](https://github.com/projectdiscovery/nuclei-templates/blob/c5be73e328ebd9a0c122ea0324f60bbdd7eb940d/http/cves/2023/CVE-2023-43177.yaml#L28) which has multiple requ

from_fact: before v3.1.4, multi-protocol templates with `flow` only returned one result
from v3.1.4, nuclei prints all events/results in a template
